content:
## English

**不審也** (bù shěn yě), meaning "did not scrutinize," denotes the fatal error of accepting information without careful verification. In the *Lüshi Chunqiu* chapter "言不可以不察" (On the Necessity of Scrutinizing Words), this phrase describes rulers who fail to examine what they hear through reliable channels. The text contrasts [[不審也]] with [[審之也]] (careful examination), using historical examples to demonstrate how the former leads to national ruin.

The passage cites King Helü of Wu and the Earl of Zhi as cautionary cases: both listened to unreliable informants—Taizhai Pi and Zhang Wu respectively—without scrutiny, ultimately causing their states' destruction and their own deaths. These examples illustrate the principle that accepting words without examination invites catastrophe.

## 中文

「不審也」者，未加詳察之謂也。《呂氏春秋·卷二十二·慎行論》言「聞而不審，不若無聞」，以明察言之要。其與「審之也」相對，蓋治國者之所以成敗者，視其聽言之慎與否焉。

夫吳王聞越王句踐於太宰嚭，智伯聞趙襄子於張武，皆不加審察，卒致國亡身死。此無他，聽言而不察其實，輕信而昧於幾也。反觀齊桓公之於管仲，楚莊王之於孫叔敖，皆能詳審而用之，故國霸諸侯。是由是觀之，察與不察，治亂之所分也。

## 日本語

「不審也」とは、よく吟味せずに受け入れることを指す。《呂氏春秋》の「言不可以不察」の篇では、この過ちが国家滅亡の原因となり得ることが示されている。信頼すべきでない情報源からの言葉を吟味もなく受け入れた吳王と智伯の例は、注意深い検討の重要性を反面教師として教えてくれる。
summary: The failure to carefully examine information, contrasted with 審之也, leading to disastrous decisions in governance.
title: Lack of Scrutiny / 不審也
